other_observers: John Vanderpoel and Roger Linfield.; Steve Bouricius banded the bird. It was seen by several reliable birders, Norm Erthal, Joey Kellner, Dick Schottler, Rich Levad, Tony Leukering, etc.
specific_location: House opposite the back of the Albertsons supermarket.
description: This was a small female plumaged hummingbird. It had a green head and back, a pale front and black wings. It had a short black bill, which was very slightly curved. It had a pale eyestripe and small post-ocular spot. It had an area of gray extending backwards and downwards from the eye, which is characteristic of Costa's, and the throat was light. The short tail had no rufous at all, was mostly green with black towards the lower part of the retrices. The outer three retrices were tipped with white. When perched on the lamp next to the feeder, it was quite easy to see that the tail and the wingtips were almost exactly the same length. This is another good fieldmark for Costa's. When at the feeder, the bird pumped its tail continuously in a regular fashion. Costa's do this in contrast with Anna's, which hold their tails relatively still.Behaviors: The bird was seen sitting at the top of a bush at on the lamp near the feeder, and drinking from the feeder. The  location was a suburban backyard in Clifton. The tail pumping was used to help separate from Anna's hummer.Call: No sounds heardPlumage: winter
